explanation: The Blackfoot disease affects the peripheral vascular system. It is endemic in nature. It is associated with the consumption of inorganic arsenic from the artesian wells. Minamata Disease– It is caused by heavily contaminated water with the toxic chemical generated in chemical factories and then discharged into the sea. Itai-Itai Disease–It is caused by Cadmium Contamination. Blue Baby Disease–It is caused by Nitrate contamination in ground water.

question: What is physical and mental dependence on drugs called
explanation: The physical and mental dependence on drugs is called Addiction. Addiction is a psychological and physical inability to consuming a chemical, drugs, activity or substance, even though it is causing psychological and physical harm.

question: Penicillin, the first antibiotic, was discovered by:
explanation: Penicillin was discovered in 1928 by Scottish Scientist Alexander Fleming. Penicillin is a group of antibiotics, derived originally from common moulds known as Penicillium moulds. Penicillin antibiotics were among the first medications to be effective against many bacterial infections caused by Staphylococci and Streptococci. • Edward Jenner :- Edward Jenner was an English Surgeon, who discovered a vaccination for small Pox. It is in fact, the world's first vaccines. • Jonas Salk :- Jonas Salk developed one of the first successful Polio Vaccines. • Louis Pasteur :- He developed vaccines for Rabies and Anthrax.

question: With regard to human blood pressure, which of the following statements is NOT correct?

options.a: The pressure of the blood inside the artery during ventricular systole (contraction) is called systolic pressure.
options.b: The pressure in the artery during the ventricular diastole (relaxation) is called diastolic pressure.
options.c: The blood pressure is much greater in arteries than in veins.
options.d: Arteries are the vessels that carry blood from various organs of the body to the heart.

explanation: Arteries are afferent (conducting or carrying towards the organs) blood vessels that carry blood away from the heart to all the organs in the body. The veins are afferent blood vessels that collect blood from the entire body and return blood to the heart. 644 / 896

question: What name is given to the fear of crossing roads?
explanation: Fear of crossing roads is called ‘Agyrophobia’. ‘Anthrophobia’ is the fear of people. ‘Atychiphobia’ is the abnormal, irrational and persistent fear of failure. ‘Aichmophobia’ is a phobia of sharp objects, pointed objects, (i.e knives scissors, needles, sharp corners and pins).

question: In old age people have to wear spectacles for reading and writing because A. their eye lenses become weak B. they lose the power of accommodation C. their eye lenses lose their magnifying power Choose the correct option.
explanation: In old age, people have to wear spectacles for reading and writing because they lose the power of accommodation.

question: Skin infections are caused by which of the following? (I) Protozoa (II) Bacteria (III) Virus
explanation: Skin infections are caused by a wide variety of germs and symptoms can very from mild to serious. Protozoa, Bacteria and Virus all causes skin infection. Skin is the largest organ of the body. Its function is to protect body from infection. Sometimes the skin itself becomes infected. Different types of bacterial skin infection include-cellulitis, impetigo, boils, leprosy. Different types of viral infection include-shingles, chickenpox, warts, measles, molluscum contagiosum.

question: Which of the following disease does NOT spread through the air?
explanation: Cholera is an infection of small intestine by some strains of the bacterium vibrio cholera. Cholera is a water borne disease. While cough, Pneumonia & tuberculosis are air borne diseases.

question: In 1956, Minamata disease was caused by which type of pollution?
explanation: Minamata disease was caused by water pollution. Minamata disease was first discovered in Minamata city in Japan, in 1956. It was caused by the release of methyl mercury in the industrial waste water from the Chisso corporations chemical factory, which continued from 1932 to 1968.

question: Which disease is caused by deficiency of Vitamin D?
explanation: Rickets is a childhood bone disorder where bones soften and becomes prone to fracture and deformity. It is rare in industrialized nations, but fairly common in some developing countries. The main cause of Rickets is a lack of Vitamin 'D'. Also not consuming enough calcium can cause Rickets.

question: Which of the following is/are agent(s) of infection?
explanation: Infections may be due to water (i.e. water borne diseases), or one to physical contacts. An infectious agent is something that infiltrates another living thing. When an infectious agent hitches a ride, that thing becomes infected host.

question: Which human organ gets majorly affected due to Vitamin D deficiency?
explanation: Body must have vitamin D to absorb calcium & promote bone growth. Too little vitamin D results in soft bones in children (Rickets) & fragile, misshapen bones in adults (Osteo malacia).

question: Which of the following diseases are due to w a t e r p o ll u t ion? (a ) C h o le r a
explanation: Cholera & Typhoid both are water inborne disease caused by the water pollution. Cholera & Typhoid are caused by bacteria. Typhoid is caused by Salmonella Typhi while Cholera is caused by the Vibrio Cholera. These are also diseases due to water pollution Diarrhea, Dysentery, Hepatitis A and Polio.

question: Which of the following disease is NOT caused by metal toxicity?
explanation: Dermatitis occurs when the skin develops an allergic reaction after being exposed to a foreign substance. This causes the body to release inflammatory chemicals that can make the skin feel itchy and irritated. • Minamata disease, sometimes referred to as Chisso- Minamata disease, is a neurological syndrome caused by severe mercury poisoning. • Itai-Itai disease was caused by cadmium poisoning due to mining in Toyama Prefecture, Japan.

question: Which disease is caused by cadmium metal pollution?
explanation: Itai–Itai disease is caused by cadmium metal pollution. It causes severe pain in spine and joints. Cadmium poisoning can also cause softening of the bones and kidney failure. While Minamata disease is caused by excess of mercury (Hg) in the body. Other diseases caused by metals are– Blue baby syndrome and black foot, which is caused by excess of nitrate and arsenic respectively. Asthma is a common inflammatery disease of the airways of lungs which is caused by mainly generic and environmental effects. Note-Dermatitis, also known as Eczema, is a group of diseases that results in inflammation of the skin.

question: Cancer treatment is done by which of the following noble gas?
explanation: Cancer is an abnormal growth of the cells which occurs through a malignant tumor. It is treated with various kinds of radio therapy or radioactive treatment. For it X-rays or radioactive substances are Rn222) used. Radon is a radioactive substance ( hence it 86 can be used for this purpose. Also the other materials used for the treatment of cancer are Cobalt-60, Arsenic 74 etc.

question: What is the cause of arise of myopia?

options.a: Excessive curvature of the eye lens.
options.c: Focal length of the eye lens becomes too long

explanation: The person suffering from Myopia can't see the distant objects but easily see the near most objects. The reason behind this is– (i) The sphericity of the eye lens is increased i.e. excessive curvature of the eye lens. (ii) The focal length of the lens decreases. (iii) Power of the eye lens increases and hence image forms in front of the retina.

question: Which of the following disease is generally caused by viruses?
explanation: • Disease cause by Viruses– Chickenpox, Smallpox, Common cold, AIDS, Measles, Mumps, Rabies, Dengu fever etc. • Disease caused by Bacteria– Diphtheria, Cholera, Leprosy, Typhoid, Tetanus, Tuberculosis, plague. • Disease caused by protozoans– Malaria, Kala-azar, Sleeping sickness. • Disease caused by worms– Filariasis, Pinworm Tapeworm.

question: Which of the following diseases are NOT caused by smog?
explanation: • Throat cancer, Rickets and breathing problems may caused by smog while the main cause of skin cancer is overexposure to sunlight, especially when it result in sunburn and blistering. UV rays from the sun Science damage DNA in your skin causing abnormal cells to form. These abnormal cells rapidally dived in a disorganized manner, forming a mass of cancer cells. • Smog is air pollution that reduces visibility. The term “Smog” was first used in early 1900s to describe a mix of smoke and fog. The smoke usually come from burning coal.

question: Which of the following controls the size of pupil in human eye?
explanation: In the human eye, behind the cornea a colour opaque membrane is located which is called Iris. In the middle of the Iris there is a sharp hole, which is called pupil. Iris controls the amount of light which enters the eye. When more light comes into it, then automatically iris compresses itself and in dark or insufficient light iris automatically expands.

question: Chemotherapy is the treatment of diseases by using
explanation: Chemotherapy is a drug treatment that uses powerful chemicals to kill fast growing cells in your body. Chemotherapy is most often used to treat cancer. Since cancer cells grow and multiply much more quickly than most cells in the body.

question: Which disease is caused by the lack of Vitamin B-1 or thiamine in the body?
explanation: Disease Vitamin Beri-Beri Vitamin-B1 Scurvy Vitamin-C Xerophthalmia or Night Blindness Vitamin-A Goiter Iodine Anemia Iron

question: Silicosis is a :
explanation: Silicosis is an interstitial lung disease caused by breathing in tiny bits of silica, a common mineral found in many types of rock and soil. Over time, exposure to silica particles causes permanent lung scarring, called Pulmonary fibrosis.

question: Country's first anti-diabetic drug, launched by the CSIR is :
explanation: The Lucknow Institute of CSIR discovered the diabetes drug 'BGR-34'. In this discovery, three scientists S. Rawat, Dr. CHV Rao and Dr. Sanjeev Kumar were awarded the Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Award. 646 / 896

question: Which of the following metals is the cause of Itai-Itai disease?
explanation: Itai-Itai disease is a well known health hazard induced by Cadmium (Cd). It was first reported in the cadmium polluted Jinzu river basin of Toyama Prefecture Japan in 1912. • The radioactive isotope, cobalt-60 is used in medical treatment of cancer and also to irradiate food. • Chromium is used to harden steel i.e. to manufacture stainless steel. • Copper is widely used in electrical industry.

question: On the basis of Which Charter, the World Cancer Day is observed?
explanation: World Cancer Day was launched in 2000 against cancer. The first World Summit was held in Paris. In this meeting, the leaders of government agencies and organizations around the world signed the Charter of Paris Against Cancer.

question: Which disease is caused by Zika Virus?
explanation: Zika virus causes microcephaly disease. Acetaminophen drug is given to relieve fever and pain due to Zika virus. The zika virus is fed by the Aedes mosquito. The Zika virus has been declared an emergency public health by the World Health Organization.

question: Which of the following projects has been launched for prevention of AIDS in the North- Eastern States of India?
explanation: Northeast states have specifically launched ‘Project Sunrise’ for AIDS prevention. Project Sunrise aims to bringing the people living with HIV / AIDS into the national mainstream and create greater awareness about the disease in these North Eastern states.

question: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists: List-I List-II A. Beri-beri 1. Vitamin A B. Haemorrhage 2. Vitamin C C. Night blindness 3. Vitamin K D. Scurvy 4. Vitamin B1 Codes: A B C D
explanation: Disease Related Vitamin Difficiency A. Beri-beri 1. Vitamin B1 B. Haemorrhage 2. Vitamin K C. Night blindness 3. Vitamin A D. Scurvy 4. Vitamin C

question: Which of the following diseases has become extinct from the world?
explanation: Small pox was an infectious disease caused by one of two virus Variola major and Variola minor. The World Health Organization Certified the global eradication of the disease in 1980.

question: Multi Drug therapy is for the infection of –
explanation: Multidrug therapy has been used to treat more than 16 million patients with leprosy during the past 20 years and has brought the global leprosy prevalence down by 96%.

question: Injection of weakened microbes to confer resistance to a disease in known as–
explanation: Vaccine is “any preparation used as preventive inoculation to confer immunity against a specific disease, usually employing an innocuous form of the disease agent as killed or weakened bacteria or viruses to stimulate antibody production.”

question: Migratory larvae of Ascaris produce symptoms of pneumonia. This is know as –
explanation: During the larval migratory phase, an acute transient pneumonitis known as Loeffler's syndrome may develop, characterised by fever cough, wheeze and marked eosinophilia.

question: Which of the following diseases is NOT water born disease?
explanation: Asthma is caused by air born allergies such as to pollen, dust mites, animal fur or feathers, smoke, fumes and pollution. The water born diseases are cholera, typhoid, diarrhea, dehydration etc.

question: Which one of the following used as anti- bacterial dye for skin infection?
explanation: Gentian violet is an antiseptic dye used to treat fungal infections of the skin (e.g. ringworm, athlete's foot). It also has weak antibacterial effects and may be used on minor cuts and scrapes to prevent infection. 647 / 896

question: If a person can see an object clearly when it is placed at about 25cm away from him, he is suffering from-
explanation: Minimum distance at which object can be seen most distinctly is called the least distance of distinct vision or near point of eye. It is 25 cm for a young adult with normal eye. • Myopia also known as 'Nearsightedness' is a very common vision disorder that is usually diagnosed before age 20. • Hypermetropia:- Hypermetropia is also known as 'Farsightedness'. A person with Hypermetropia can see distant objects clearly but cannot see nearby objects distinctly. • Astigmatism is a common and generally treatable imperfection in the curvature of the eye that causes blurred distance and near vision.

question: A scientist was treating pathogenic bacteria with different chemicals to see which of them would stop the activity of their ribosome. Which one of the following processes is the target?
explanation: DNA synthesis is the natural or artificial creation of deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) molecules. DNA synthesis occurs in all eukaryotes and prokaryotes, as well as some viruses. The accurate synthesis of DNA is important in order to avoid mutation to DNA.

question: Prolonged consumption of pigeon pea (Arhar or Tur dal) adulterated with kesari dal (Lathyrus) sativus leads to-
explanation: Prolonged consumption of pigeon pea (Arhar or Tur dal) adulterated with kesari dal (Lathyrus) sativus leads to paralysis. India prohibited the sale and storage of Kesari in any form since 1961, based on suggestions made by scientist and epidemiologists that lathyrus consumed in high amount for prolonged periods could cause lathyrism-a condition that could lead to paralysis.

question: The organ, of the body which is mainly damaged in hepatitis, is-
explanation: Hepatitis means inflammation of the liver. The liver is a vital organ that processes nutrients, filters the blood, and fights infections. When the liver inflamed or damaged, its function can be affected. Heavy Alcohol use, toxins, some medications, and certain medical conditions can cause Hepatitis. The most common types of Hepatitis are ‘Hepatitis A’, ‘Hepatitis B’, and ‘Hepatitis C’. Science Hepatitis A is usually a short term infection. Hepatitis B and C can cause long-term or chronic infections. A person can have both Hepatitis B and Hepatitis C at the same time.

question: Which of the following diseases is/are likely to spread in the entire community if a leaky septic tank contaminates the water supply? 1. Cholera 2. Typhoid 3. Tuberculosis 4. Leprosy Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
explanation: Cholera is an acute diarrhea illness caused by infection of the intestine with Vibrio Cholera Bacteria. People can get sick when they swallow food or water contaminated with Cholera Bacteria. Typhoid fever is also an infection that spreads through contaminated food and water. People who drink contaminated water or eat food washed in contaminated water can develop Typhoid fever.

question: Anti-rabies vaccine was discovered by-
explanation: Anti Rabies vaccine was discovered by Louis Pasteur and Emile Roux in 1885. • Edward Jenner was a British Physician and scientist who discovered, the world's first vaccine for smallpox. • Robert Koch was a German Physician and Microbiologist who of specific causative agents of deadly infectious diseases including tuberculosis, cholera.

question: Iodization of salt is a public health measure to prevent
explanation: The ingestion of iodine mixed salt prevents iodine deficiency and is the leading preventable cause of goiter, swelling of the thyroid gland. Iodine deficiency of goiter is a major public health problem that can be cheaply addressed by purposely adding small amounts of iodine the sodium chloride salt.

question: Overcrowding and defective ventilation cause-
explanation: Overcrowding and defective ventilation cause air-borne disease. If you live in an overcrowded and poorly ventilated house it is possible that you may suffer from some air borne diseases. The chances of spreading the air borne diseases are more in the crowded and poorly ventilated area, this is because in such areas, the person infectious with some diseases easily spreads the infection droplets through breathing and sneezing. 648 / 896

question: Children confined to closed dark rooms are most likely to suffer from
explanation: Children confined to closed dark rooms are most likely to suffer from bone deformation. In children, vitamin D deficiency also result in a range of bone deformations such as rickets.

question: Which one of the following is a viral disease?
explanation: Influenza is a viral infection that attacks respiratory system like nose, throat and lungs. It is generally known as "flu" viruses that cause Diarrhea and vomiting.

question: Consider the following pairs : Disease Causative organism 1. Ascariasis Bacteria 2. Hepatitis Virus 3. Tetanus Protozoa Which of the above pairs is/are correctly matched?
explanation: Ascariasis is an infection of the Small intestine caused by Ascaris lumbricoides, which is a Species of roundworm. • Hepatitis means Inflammation of the liver, when the liver is inflamed or damaged, its function can be affected. Heavy alcohol use, toxins, some medications and certain medical conditions can cause hepatitis, However, hepatitis is often caused by a virus. • Tetanus is caused by a bacterium called Clostridium tetani.

question: Pneumoconiosis is a disease produced by-
explanation: Pneumoconiosis is a lung disease caused by breathing in certain kinds of dust particles that damage human lungs. It is caused by breathing in coal dust.

question: Which pollutant in water causes 'Blue baby syndrome' ?
explanation: The most common cause of 'Blue Baby syndrome' is water contaminated with nitrates. After a body drinks formula made with nitrate-rich water the body converts the nitrates into nitrites. These nitrites bind to the hemoglobin in the body forming methemoglobin which is unable to carry oxygen.

question: If the drinking water supply pipeline is damaged and is contaminated with domestic sewerage, which one of the following diseases is most likely to occur after drinking such water?
explanation: The goal of disinfection of public water supplies is the elimination of the pathogens that are responsible for water borne diseases. The transmission of diseases such as Typhoid and Paratyphoid fevers, cholera, Salmonellosis and Shigellosis can be controlled with treatments that substantially reduce the total number of viable microorganisms in the water.

question: Which one of the following is the vector for kala-azar disease?
explanation: There is only one sandfly vector of Kala- azar in India which is Phlebotomus argentipes. Sandflies are small insects about one fourth of a mosquito.

question: Which one of the following diseases is caused by bacteria?
explanation: Tuberculosis is an air-borne disease caused by the bacterial species mycobacterium tuberculosis that effect the lungs. It is a curable and preventable disease. But it is a contagious disease that spread from person to person through air. When people with TB Cough sneeze of spit, the germs are released into the air. Thus any person coming in contacts in inhaling the air become infected. The symptoms are cough, night sweats, weight loss etc.

question: Which of the following are infected by 'foot- and-mouth disease?
explanation: Foot and mouth disease (FMD) or hoof and mouth disease (HMD) is an infectious and sometimes fatal viral disease that affects cloven - hoofed animals including domestic and wild bovids. Susceptible animals include cattle, camels, buffalo, sheep, goats, pigs deer and bison.

question: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists: List-I List-II (Deficiency) (Cause) A. Beri-beri 1. Vitamin C deficiency B. Pellagra 2. Niacin deficiency Pernicious Thiamine deficiency C. 3. anemia D. Scurvy 4. Vitamin B deficiency 12 5. Vitamin A deficiency Code: A B C D
explanation: Beriberi – Thiamine Deficiency (Vitamin B ) 1 Pellagra – Niacin Deficiency (Vitamin B ) 3 Pernicious – Vitamin B Deficiency 12 Anemia (Cyanocobalamin) Scurvy – Vitamin C deficiency (Ascorbic acid) 649 / 896

question: Kala-Azar is most prevalent in which of the following states ?
explanation: Kala-Azar is also known as Black Fever. A disease caused by infection with Leishmania Parasites. Some people have no symptoms. For other, symptoms may include fever, weight loss and swelling of the spleen or liver. Bihar has more than 70% of the disease burden, with Kala Azar endemic in 33 of its 38 districts.

question: The chemical identified to trap malaria mosquitoes is :
explanation: The chemical Cedrol, found in mosquito breeding sites near African lake Victoria, could be used in traps that would attract and kill the female mosquito, preventing reproduction before she lays hundreds of eggs.

question: Goitre is developed as a result of lack of ––––– in the thyroid gland.
explanation: National Institute of Communicable Disease is situated at New Delhi. It aims to keep you updated about the communicable diseases. It is an institute of various areas of training and research using multi-disciplinary integrated approach It is to be expected to provide expertise to the States/UTs on rapid health assessment.

question: The substances used to bring down body temperature in high fever is
explanation: An antipyretic is a substance that reduces fever. Antipyretics cause the hypothalamus to override a prostaglandin – induced increase in temperature. The body then works to lower the temperature, which results in a reduction in fever.

question: Which one of the following causes Chagas Disease?
explanation: Chagas disease is caused by the parasite Trypanosoma cruzi. • Parasite trypanosoma cruzi is spread form an insect known as the Triatomine bug, or "kissing bug". • These insect can become infected by parasite when they swallow blood from an animal that is infected with parasite. • Triatomine bugs live primarily in mud, adobe huts in Mexico, South America and Central America.

question: Smoking injures
explanation: Smoking can cause lung disease by damaging your airways and the small air sacs (alveoli) found in lungs. • Lung diseases caused by smoking include COPD, which includes emphysema and chronic bronchitis. • If any person have Asthama, Tobacco smoke can trigger an attack or make an attack worse. • Smokers are 12 to 13 times more likely to die from COPD than nonsmokers.

question: Which of the following is not a parasitic disease
explanation: Tuberculosis is not a parasitic disease but it is caused by a bacteria called Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Tuberculosis disease is mainly affects the lungs. Filariasis, Leishmaniasis, Malaria are caused by a parasite. A parasitic disease is an infectious diseases caused or transmitted by a parasite. The study of parasitic disease is called Parasitology.

question: The Test that identifies Sugar levels in human blood is
explanation: The HbA1C is a blood test that provides information about your average levels of blood glucose, also called blood sugar. The A1C test can be used to diagnose type 2 diabetics and prediabetes. The A1C test is also the primary test used for diabetes management. The A1C test is also called the hemoglobin HbA1C, glycated hemoglobin and glycohemoglobin test.

question: Excess nitrates in drinking water causes
explanation: Excess nitrates in drinking water causes Methemoglobinemia (also known as blue baby syndrome). Standard for nitrate in drinking water is 10 milligrams per liter. Ingestion of excess fluoride in drinking water can cause fluorosis which affects the teeth and bones. Anemia can have many causes including dietary deficiency lack of iron, Vitamin B12 or folic acid in the diet. 650 / 896

question: Eradication of fresh water breeding day biting mosquito will prevent which of the following disease?
explanation: The Dengue virus is spread through a human to Mosquito-to-human cycle of transmission, with the Mosquito Aedes Aegypti as the primary vector. Eradication of fresh water breading day biting Mosquito will prevent Dengue Disease. There is no specific treatment for dengue fever. Fever reducers and pain killers can be taken to control the symptoms of muscle aches and pains and fever. The best options to treat these symptoms are acetaminophen or Paracetamol.

question: Bacterial diseases syphilis is caused by-
explanation: The cause of syphilis is a bacterium called Treponema Pallidum. The most common route of transmission is through contact with an infected person's sore during sexual activity. The Bacteria enter your body through minor cuts or abrasions in your skin or mucous membrane.

question: Typhoid fever is an extremely serious infection of the gastrointestinal tract. It is caused by :
explanation: Typhoid fever is caused by the highly contagious salmonella Typhi bacteria. The Bacteria spread through contaminated food or water they multiply and spread in bloodstream. Symptoms are prolonged fever, headache, Nausea. Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by a bacterium called Mycobacterium tuberculosis. The bacteria usually attacks the lungs.

question: Identify the wrong statement.

options.a: The main component of kidney stones is calcium oxalate.
options.b: Bile juice helps in digestion of lipids.
options.c: The liquid portion of blood is called plasma
options.d: The organisms that can make their own food are called heterotrophs.

explanation: Calcium oxalate are the most common type of kidney stone. Bile helps with digestion. It breaks down fats into fatty acids which can be taken into the body by the digestive tract. The liquid part of blood is called Plasma, made of water salts and protein. A heterotroph is an organism that eats other plants or animals for energy and nutrients. The organisms that can make their own food are called Autotrophs. Science

question: Which of the following is NOT related to cancer?
explanation: Insulin is not related to cancer. It is considered as the main anabolic hormone of the body. It regulates the metabolism of carbohydrates, fats and protein by promoting the absorption of glucose from the blood into liver fat and skeletal muscle cells. When insulin increase in blood leads to abnormality low blood glucose level called Hypoglycemia. Decreasing the insulin cause blood sugar. Biopsy, Tumour, Chemotherapy are related to cancer.

question: Which of the following diseases is not spread by mosquitoes?
explanation: Polio or poliomyelitis is a disabling and life threatening disease caused by the Poliovirus. The virus spread from person to person and can infect a person's spinal cord, causing paralysis. Its symptoms are muscle weakness resulting in an inability to move. The number of poliomyelitis cases have been reduced by over 99%.

question: Which one of the following causes dropsy?
explanation: Epidemic dropsy is a form of edema of extremities due to poisoning by Argemone Mexicana (Mexican prickly poppy). Epidemic dropsy is a clinical state resulting from use of edible oils adulterated with Argemone Mexicana seed oil.

explanation: Many diseases which are spread through the air : (1) T. B. (Tuberculosis) (2) Mumps (Paramyxovirus) (3) Measles (Rubeola virus) (4) Whooping cough (pertussis) (5) The common cold (6) Influenza (7) Corona virus (COVID 19).

question: Multi-Drug-Therapy (MDT) is used for the treatment of disease-
explanation: Leprosy is curable with a combination of drugs known as Multidrug Therapy (MDT), as the treatment of leprosy with only one antileprosy drug (monotherapy) will result in development of drug resistance to that drug. question: Dengue is transmitted by a mosquito called-
explanation: Dengue is transmitted by a mosquito called Aedes aegypti mosquito (female mosquito). Dengue viruses are spread to people through the bites of infected Aedes species mosquitoes.

question: Hypoglycemia, glycosuria and polyuria are caused by the deficiency of-

question: In acquired immunodeficiency syndrome, HIV attacks on _____ (1) Red blood cells (2) Lymphocytes (3) T cells (4) White blood cells Select the correct answer using the codes below:
explanation: HIV cells on entering the body, Attack and destroy the Helper T-cells. Thus the immunity of the body is reduced in HIV infected persons. T-Helper cells also known as CD4 cells are white blood cells which protect our body from infection. When any virus or Bacteria attacks the body, these cells becomes active and provides immunity to the body. White blood cells help fight infection and defend the body against other foreign materials. T cells are born from hematopoietic stem cells, found in the bone marrow. • Some autoimmune disorders can kill WBCs or bone marrow stem cells, which make blood cells and can lead to leukopenia. • Autoimmune hemolytic anemia is rare red blood cell disorder and an immune disorder when the body produces antibodies that destroy the red blood cells. • Lymphocytes are type of white blood cell (or leukocyte). They help an organism to fight infections.

question: Consider the following statements: a. Kwashiorkor is a disorder of children due to protein/essential acid deficiency. b. AIDS can be transferred from mother to child through placenta. Which of the above statements is/are correct? Select the code for the correct answer from the options given below:
explanation: The Protein Energy Malnutrition (PEM) term is a group of related disorders that include Kwashiorkor and intermediate stages of Marasmus Kwashiorkor. It mostly affects children, most often around the age of weaning through age 5. Kwashiorkor is a disease marked by severe protein malnutrition and bilateral extremity swelling. • Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome (AIDs) is an infection caused by the human immunodeficiency virus. During pregnancy, the foetus is infected by HIV crossing the Placenta. At the time of birth, the baby can get infected by HIV in the mother’s cervical secretions or blood.

question: Shortage of which food item causes the Goitre Disease?
explanation: Iodine deficiency can lead to enlargement of the thyroid (Goiter disease) Element Iodine an essential nutrient in the diet. It may result in metabolic problems such as goiter. Sometimes as an endemic goiter as well as Congential iodine deficiency syndrome due to untreated congenital hypothyroidism which results in developmental delays and other health problems.

question: The full form of HIV is-
explanation: The full form of HIV is the Human Immunodeficiency Virus. HIV is a virus that attacks cells that help the body to fight infection, making a person more vulnerable to other infections and diseases. HIV causes AIDs (Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome) the virus can be transmitted through contact with infected blood, semen or vaginal fluids.

question: Cerebral malaria is caused by-
explanation: Cerebral malaria is the most severe neurological complication of infection with Plasmodium falciparum malaria. It is a clinical syndrome characterized by coma and a sexual forms of the parasite on peripheral blood smears.

question: Spraying of oil on stagnant water controls malaria because the

options.a: Oil kills malarial parasites in mosquitoes

explanation: The Oil kills Anopheles mosquitoes due to entry into and blocking of the respiratory tract. Stagnant water is the breeding ground for Mosquitoes. If oil is sprayed on stagnant water, it will form a thin layer of oil on the surface. The Mosquito larva in the water will not be able to breathe and they will die. 652 / 896

question: Who discovered that mosquito served as a carrier of malaria?
explanation: Sir Ronald Ross discovered the Malaria Parasite in the gastrointestinal tract of mosquito and led to the realization, that malaria was transmitted by Mosquitoes. He received the Nobel Prize in 1902 for his Work on Malaria. Jonas Edward Salk was an American Virologist who developed one of the first successful Polio vaccines. Louis Pasteur was a French Chemist and Microbiologist renowned for his discoveries of the Principles of Vaccination, Microbial fermentation and Pasteurization. Robert Koch was a German physician and Microbiologist. He is regarded as one of the main founders of Modern Bacteriology along with the Father of MicroBiology (with Louis Pasteur) and Father of Medical bacteriology.

question: Another name for Myopia is :
explanation: The other name for Myopia is short- sightedness. It is a condition in which close object appear clearly, but far ones does not. Sightedness is corrected using a concave lens. Long sightedness (or) Hypermyopia is the inability to see near objects clearly. This defect arises when the image is formed behind the retina. This defect may arise due to shortening of eye ball. This defect is corrected by wearing spectacles with convex.

question: The biggest flower in the world is
explanation: The flower with the worlds largest bloom is the Rafflesia Arnoldii. This rare flower is found in the rainforests of Indonesia. It can grow upto 3 feet across and weight upto 15 pounds (6.80 kg). It is also found in South East Asia Rafflesia are most common in Borneo and Sumatra. It is a parasitic plant. 653 / 896

question: “Amrapali” variety of mango is a cross of
explanation: The Amrapali mango is a named mango cultivar introduced in 1971. It was developed as a hybrid variety of Dasheri and Neelam by Dr. Pijush Kanti Majumdar at the Indian Agriculture Research Institute in Delhi.

question: What is hydroponics?
explanation: Hydroponics is type of horticulture and a subset of hydro culture which involves growing plants, usually crops without soil by using mineral nutrients solutions in on aqueous solvent.

question: Which of the following is called the amphibian of the plant kingdom?
explanation: Amphibians are the organisms that live on both land and in water. Bryophytes are called Amphibians of the plant kingdom because these plant, live in soil, but they need water for asexual reproduction. The sperm of bryophyte are flagellate and need water to swim to the egg. In other words, as these plants need water for reproduction unlike other plant they are called as Amphibians.

question: In pineapple and jackfruit, edible part is
explanation: In pineapple, the edible part is peduncle, perianth and pericarps. In jackfruit, the perianth is the edible part. The ovaries of the flowers forming the pinepple are not conspicuous and all the parts of the flowers contribute to the formation of the entire fruit. Science

question: During the process of photosynthesis, which of the following type of energy is absorbed by chlorophyll.
explanation: In Photosynthesis, Chlorophyll absorbs light energy to transform carbon dioxide and water into carbohydrates and oxygen. • This is the process that converts solar energy to a form that can be utilized by plants and by the animals that eat them, to form the foundation of the food chain. • The absorption of light energy and its conversion into chemical energy occurs in multi protein complexes.

question: The cell wall of a plant is composed of:
explanation: The cell wall is the outermost layers of a plant cell. It is thick, strong, perforated and inanimate. It is mainly made of Cellulose. Note :– Cellulose is a complex substance that provides structural stability to plant cells.

question: What happens due to the Guttation process in the plants?

options.b: Excess water is released from plants through leaves.
options.c: Dead plant cells are replaced by newer ones.

explanation: Guttation is when a plant pushes water out through tips of the leaves of plants. These are special pores called hydathodes. The process of guttation occurs when the roots of a plant absorb water from the soil. This creates a pressure in side the plant, and the water is forced out through the hydathodes.

question: Which one of the following plants grows naturally in a saline environment?
explanation: Halophyte:- Plant that grow in High Saline Halophytes. Water are called Halophytic plants or • They are found mainly in mangrove swamps, underlandes ocean and ocean cossets salt water lakes and saline areas. • Halophytes constitute only 2% of the total vegetative composition on earth. Some Important Halophytes:- Salicornia Europaea, Sporobolus alterni florus, Atriplex Halimus.

question: The study of grasses is known as:
explanation: Agrostology, the branch of botany, is concerned with the study of grasses, especially their classification. Agronomy is the study of intersection of crop genetics. Anthology is the study of flowers and flowering plants. Cell Biology is the study of cell structure and functions. 654 / 896

question: The part of Aloe vera plant used in cosmetics and medicinal plants is
explanation: Aloe vera, a cactus-like plant has been used for traditional medical purposes for thousands of years. Aloe leaves can be separated into two basic products.

question: The latex, a bitter yellow liquid beneath the epidermis of the leaf. 2. The gel, a colourless and bitter substance in the inner part of the leaf. 1582. Plants attached to stones and rocks are known as :
explanation: Lithophytes are plants that grow in or on rocks. These plants that grow on rocks are also known as epipetric or epilithic plant. Halophyte is a salt-tolerant plant that grows in soil or waters of high salinity coming into contact with soline water through its root or by salt spray such as in saline semi- deserts, mangrove swamps, marshes and sloughs, and seashores. Aerophytes, a plant growing entirely in the air and receiving its nourishment from it. Psammophyte, a plant that thrives in shifting sands, primarily in desert. Psammophytes are marked by a number of adaptations, that enable them to exist on wind blown sands.

question: A vegetable which is also known as flower is
explanation: Broccoli is a kind of Mini Cabbage Grown for its florets (Flower head). Broccoli is an edible green plant in the cabbage family whose large almost flowering head is eaten as a vegetable. The word broccoli means "The flowering crest of a cabbage". The Broccoli can be boiled, baked or steamed and can also be eaten raw.

question: Match the following : List-I List-II (A) Equisetum 1. Water fern (B) Marsilea 2. Horse tail (C) Cyathea 3. Clubmoss (D) Lycopodium 4. Tree fern Code : (A) (B) (C) (D)
explanation: (A) Equisetum Horse tail (B) Marsilea Water fern (C) Cyathea Tree fern (D) Lycopodium Clubmoss Equisetum–Equisetum is the only living genus in Equisetaceae, a family of vascular plants that reproduce by spores rather than seeds. Science Marsilea–Marsilea is a genus of approximately 65 species of aquatic ferns of the family marsileaceae. Cyathea–Cyathea is a genus of tree ferns, the type genus of the fern order cyatheales. The genus name cyathea is derived from the Greek Kyatheion, meaning "Little Cup". Lycopodium–Lycopodium is a genus of club mosses, also known as ground pines or creeping cedars, in the family Lycopodiaceae.

question: Which plant is an excellent example for autonomic movement of variation?
explanation: Desmodium gyrans are also known as Telegraph plant. The leaf of the Telegraph plant move as antennas for the most optimal position to receive light. This happens so quickly, that it is visible to the naked eye. The plant remain small and can grow on a place in semi-shade at a minimum of 100C.

question: Which of following pairs is matched INCORRECTLY?
explanation: (cid:2) Flowering plants are Angiosperms have seeds encased in remnants of the flower. (cid:2) Non-flowering plants are Gymnosperms means 'naked seeds'.

question: Pinus tree is an example of ______.
explanation: Pinus tree is an example of Gymnosperm. Gymnosperm are a group of seed-producing plants that includes conifers, cycads, Ginkgo, and Gnetophytes. Gymnosperms have major economic uses. Pine, fir, spruce and cedar are all example of conifers that are used for lumber, paper production and resin.

question: Which of the following weeds is commonly known as 'Gajar Ghas' or 'Congress Grass'?
explanation: Parthenium is commonly known as “ Gajar Ghas” or “Congress Grass”. It is most common invasive species in India. Parthenium hysterophorus plant causes milk disease in livestock and also responsible for respiratory malfunction in humans.

question: Dendrology is the study of:
explanation: • Dendrology−Study of Woody Plants (Shrubs and Trees) • It is the scientific study of the morphological characteristic of Woody Plants, their relationship Biological characteristics, ecological requirements variability, distribution and economic importance • Pedology−Study of soil. 655 / 896

question: Which of the following is NOT present in a matured stomata?
explanation: Tartaric acid (C H O ) is an organic 4 6 6 compound which is naturally found in many plants. It is found in excess in grapes and tamarind. • Oxalic acid (C H O ) is a dicarboxylic acid also 2 2 4 found in the for m of potassium salts in vegetations. It is also found in the term of salt of potassium– hydrogen in the form of plants as Oxalis and Rumex. It is also found in the stone in the kidney. • Formic acid is basically found in red ants and bees this is used as a insecticide, in the form of medicine of arthritis, in the manufacturing of rubber leather, cloth etc. • Ascorbic acid is an Organic acid soluble in water and found in citrus fruits. It is also called 'Vitamin C'.

question: Which one of the following is the major characteristic for deciduous trees ?

options.a: They do not lose their leaves.
options.d: They depend on other factors for their food.

explanation: • Deciduous is a term that refers to trees which seasonally shed their unnecessary parts, such as leaves, from their structure. Most deciduous trees are broad leaf trees. Deciduous trees are more sensitive to temperature and rainfall. The soil is usually rich. • Deciduous trees are generally seen in the deciduous forests of India. Majority of Indian forest comes under this category. This forest shed their leaves during dry periods to reduced the rate of transpiration i.e. body. Ex. Oak and Maple.

question: Which is the usual reproductive part of a plant?
explanation: A flower, sometimes known as a bloom or blossom is the reproductive structure found in flowering plants. The biological function of a flower is to facilitate reproduction, usually by providing a mechanism for the union of sperm & eggs.

question: In which condition, tree is most vulnerable to die?

options.b: During loss of its all leaves
options.c: During loss of half of branches
options.d: During loss of half of leaves Science

explanation: Tree is most vulnerable to die during loss of its all leaves. As the leaves of a plant comes in various shapes and sizes and they are vital to a plant's existence as they play one of the most important functions. Most plants are capable of making their own food but would be unable to do this without leaves. Food is produced in a plant by a simple process called photosynthesis.

question: Match the structures given in column (A) with the functions given in column (B). Column–A Column-B I. Stomata 1. Absorption of water II. Xylem 2. Transpiration III. Root hairs 3. Transport of food IV. Phloem 4. Transport of water 5. Synthesis of carbohydrates

question: By its which part Bryophyllum can reproduce?
explanation: Bryophyllum are also known as air plants. It reproduce by its leaves. Bryophyllum, reproduction occurs through asexual means, specifically, through vegetative propagation of leaves. The leaves of Bryophyllum are broad possessing notches at its margin, these are the locations from which buds (notches) arise. The newly formed plants can then detach from the leaves to grow into a mature plant once it touches the soil.

question: Identify the legume crop which does not fix atmospheric nitrogen?
explanation: Rhizobium bacteria found in the roots (saplings) of pulses provide them to the plants by rationalizing the free nitrogen in the atmosphere. For this reason, these crops do not require much nitrogen. These crops are- gram, pigeon pea, moong, urad, peas, soyabean etc., while Rajma does not fix the atmospheric nitrogen, whereas fodder crops like Berseem and Rijka also have high protein accumulation, that maintains the fertility of the land.

question: Which one of the following pairs is NOT correctly matched? Chemical Uses

options.c: Insecticides Used against avian and ants
options.d: Antibiotics Used against bacterial plant pathogens 656 / 896

explanation: Herbicides – To kills weeds Fungicides – Used against fungal infections Insecticides – Used against avian and ants Antibiotics – Used against bacterial infections in people and animals.

question: Essential element present in Chlorophyll is:
explanation: Chlorophyll– • It is a green-colored pigment that is found in plants and helps in photosynthesis. • For the synthesis of chlorophyll in plants the elements needed are iron and magnesium. • Magnesium is a mobile and essential element that is important for the growth and development of a plant. Chlorophyll also has medicinal uses– • Detoxification, wound healing, reducing bed breath, and treating constipation.

question: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer from the code given below the lists: List-I List-II A. Brahmi 1. Antidiabetic B. Sadabahar 2. Carminative C. Pudina 3. Expectorant D. Tulsi 4. Brain Tonic Code: A B C D
explanation: The correct match is as follows- Drug Related Disorders Brahmi Brain tonic Sadabahar Anti Diabetic Pudina Carminative Tulsi Expectorant

question: The yellow colour of mangoes is due to the presence of
explanation: • The deep yellow color of mangoes is due to the presence of beta carotene a common phytochemical within a group of 600 known as Carotenoids. • Chlorophyll is a pigment that gives plants their green color, and it helps plants to create their own food through photosynthesis. • Anthoxanthins– Anthoxanthins are water-soluble pigments which range in color from white or colorless to a creamy to yellow, often on petals of flowers. • Anthocyanin: Anthocyanins are water-soluble vacuolar pigments that, depending on their pH, may appear red, purple, blue or black.

question: Most primitive living vascular plant are
explanation: • Pteridophytes are primitive vascular plants, commonly known as ferns. Unlike other vascular plants, the life cycle of ferns is split between free- living gametophyte and sporophytes phases. • Ferns are the member of a bunch of vascular plants that reproduce via spores and have neither seeds nor Flowers. • Brown algae are the member of Multicellular Algae. • Cycas belongs to the family of Cycadaceae • Sphagnum is a genus and also known as peat moss.

question: Temporary wilting occurs in plants due to
explanation: • The rate of loss of water from the plant is greater than the absorption of water in the plant. This usually occurs in low humid and very large temperature conditions. The rate of transpiration is very large for the plants. • Temporary wilting occurs when the turgor pressure in non-lignified plant cells falls towards zero, as a result of diminished water in the cells.

question: Which of the following is a triploid plant ?
explanation: Banana is a Triploid plant. They have three sets of chromosomes and many desirable characteristics. Triploid organisms are normally sterile as their lack of homologous chromosomes prevents pairing during meiosis. This can be useful to plant breeders, for example in banana culmination: sterile triploid bananas can be propagated a sexually and will not contain any seeds.

question: Plants are green because of the presence of a pigment called :
explanation: Chlorophyll is a pigment that gives plants their green color, and it helps plants to create their own food through photosynthesis.

question: Which of the following plant is grown for the reclamation of ravines ?
explanation: Concentrated runoff after rains from rills, which with intense rains, due to concentrated runoff gets converted into gullies. Gullies get deeper and deeper and longer with time forming ravines. • Eucalyptus Globulus, commonly known as southern blue gum, is a species of tall, evergreen tree endemic to Southeastern Australia. • Prosopis Juliflora is a shrub or small tree in the family Fabaceae, a kind of mesquite. It is native to Mexico, South America and the Caribbean Sea. 657 / 896 • Dalbergia Sissoo, known commonly as North Indian rosewood or shisham, is a fast-growing, hardy, deciduous rosewood tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southern Iran. Dalbergia Sissoo is a large, crooked tree with long, leathery leaves and whitish or pink flowers.

question: What type of fruit is pineapple?
explanation: Pineapple is a composite type of fruit. It is known as sorosis type. It develops from an entire inflorescence, from a cluster of group of flowers into an entire fruit. Edible parts of a Sorosis type of fruit are Perianth and peduncle. A syconus fruit is a composite fruit which takes form a hollow fleshy inflorescence stalk. Within this structure development of tiny flower is seen. This fruit develops from hypanthodium inflorescence.

question: Strobilus is a structure associated with –
explanation: A strobilus is present on many land plant species consisting of sporangia bearing structures densely aggregated along a stem. They are often called cones. Pinus are associated with strobilus.

question: The plant introduced form the “new world” to “old world” is
explanation: Christopher Columbus introduced certain animals (horses), sugar plant and diseases to the new world from the old world while introducing certain commodities like sugar, tobacco, potato from the new world to the old world. These plant were introduced to the old world to change the cuisine there.

question: The part of the poppy plant which yields opium is
explanation: The part of the poppy plant which yields opium is dried seeds. Approximately 12 percent of opium is made up of the analgesic alkaloid morphine which is processed chemically to produce heroin and other synthetic opioids for medicinal use and for the illegal drug trade. Main producers of opium:- Afghanistan (primary), Myanmar, Colombia, Laos, Mexico, India, Pakistan, Thailand, Turkey, China, Australia etc.

question: Which one of the following pigments in plants is involved in the perception of photoperiodie stimuli, which control flowering, seed germination and other morphogenetic phenomena?
explanation: Phytochrome is a blue-green plant pigment which regulates plant development, including seed germination, stem growth, leaf expansion, pigment synthesis and flowering. • Phytochrome is responsible for the absorption of red and far-red light and it is involved in red far red light inter conversion. • Photoperidism is the effect of duration of light and darkness on the growth, development and behavior of organisms. • Compound Florien is synthesized in photoperidism which is responsible for flowering plant.

question: The edible part of potato is :
explanation: The edible part of potato is stem. The potatoes grow on underground stem called, Stolons. Potato is a modified stem called a tuber. It stores a large amount of edible starch which is used as food. Potatoes are rich in Fibers, Potassium, Vitamin C and Vitamin B6.

question: Penicillin was invented by
explanation: Alexander Flemming was a Scottish Physician-scientist who discovered Penicillin. • Alfred Nobel invented Dynamite. • Dr. Christian Barnard designed artificial heart valves. He is known as the first surgeon to perform human to human heart transplant action.

question: Which of the following plants is a Source of biodiesel?
explanation: Oils from Jatropha, melon, palm oil, soybean, sugarcane and used oil can be used to make biodiesel. • Jatropha is very important in the production of biodiesel whereas it is a non edible plant.

question: Which of the following is a plant hormone?
explanation: Cytokinin is the plant hormone which has a major role in growth and development of the plants. It helps in embryogenesis, cell division, chloroplast differentiation and many other important role in plants. Plant produce various type of hormones including. • Auxin • Cytokinin (CK) • Abscisic Acid (ABA) • Gibberellins (GA) • Ethylene (ET) • Salicyclic Acid (SA) • Jasmonates (JA) • Peptides etc. • Insulin, Oestrogen, Thyroxin etc. are human harmones. 658 / 896

question: Consider the following statements and choose the correct answer from the options given below : There are no special organs of excretion in plants because : I. Plant use all the substances that enter their body completely II. Plants break down substances very slowly when compared to animals III. Waste products are stored in leaves that fall off IV. Waste products are exuded in the form of resins, saps and latexes

options.a: I is correct and II and III are wrong
options.b: I and II are correct and III and IV are wrong
options.c: II and III are correct and I and IV are wrong
options.d: I is wrong and II-IV are correct

explanation: The process of excretion in plants is quite simple. Hence excretory organs are not found in plants. Some Reasons are as Fallows- • The rate of catabolism in plants is much lower than the animals. As a result the metabolic waste accumulates in the plant's body at a much slower rate. • Green plants use much of the wastes of catabolism in their anabolic processes. For example water and CO 2 produced in respiration is used in photosynthesis. • Inland plants, CO , oxygen and water vapour are 2 given out through stomata or lenticels.

question: Which one of the following phenomenon means "acquisition of the ability to flower by chilling treatment"?
explanation: Vernalization is the acquisition of a plants ability to flower in the spring and winters by exposure to the prolonged cold of winter. Vernalization is defined as the method of inducing early flowering in plants by treatment of their seeds at a very low temperature.

question: Stamens are branched in which of the following plants?
explanation: The Castor plant is a robust annual that may grow 6 to 15 feet (2-5 meter) in one season with full sunlight, heat and adequate moisture. In areas with mild, frost-free winters it may live for many years and become quite woody and tree-like. Stamens are branched of castor bean plant (Ricinus communis).

question: Which is known as "Sundew Plant"?
explanation: Drosera, known as Sundew Plant is insectivorous plant with mucilaginous substance covering the leaf surface that resembles drops of morning dew. • Cuscuta plant is called a parasite because it does not have chlorophyll and absorbs food material from the host. Science • Viscum is a genus of about 70-100 species of mistletoes, native to temperate and tropical regions of Europe, Africa, Asia and Australia. • Vanda, abbreviated in the horticultural trade as V is a genus in the orchid family, Orchidaceae.

question: Which plant group is commonly known as amphibians of plant kingdom?
explanation: Amphibians are certain species that live both on earth and in water. Bryophytes are called plant kingdom amphibians even though those plants live in soil and they require water for sexual reproduction. The bryophyte sperm, which is antherozoids is flagellate and requires water to swim into the eggs. They have rhizoids instead of root which help the plant to anchor to the Surface.

question: Who proposed natural system of plant's classification?
explanation: Natural System is a system of classification based on natural similarities of vegetative & floral characters. Example:- George Bentham & Joseph Dalton Hooker classified plants into: • Cryptogams (non flowering plants) • Phanerogams (seed bearing plants)

question: Which part is absent in Gymnosperm?
explanation: Ovary - • Gymnosperms are plants that produce seeds but lack an ovary. • The seeds in gymnosperm plants are naked. • There are exposed seeds due to the overies, so do not bring fruit. Embryo • The diploid zygote develops into an embryo while still enclosed within the ovule and the ovule becomes a seed.

question: Which of the following statements about plant cells are true? I. Plant cells contain centrioles. II. Plant cells have chloroplasts. III. Plant cells possess cells walls. IV. Plant cells have small vacuoles.
explanation: Plant cells do not contain centrioles. Centrioles are present only in animal cells and in some lower plant cells. But in all higher plant cells, centrioles are absent. Plant cells have chloroplasts because chloroplasts are found in all green plants and algae and they are food producers of plants, found in the guard cells located in the leaves of the plants. 659 / 896 Plant cells possess cells walls present adjacent to the cell membrane, which is also called the plasama membrane. Plant cells have large-sized vacuoles than animal cells because they occupy upto 90% of the cell’s volume and need to store food and water, that is why the plant does not possess the ability to move free like animals.

question: Which organ of a plant performs respiration?
explanation: Leaf– Leaves possess small opening called as stomata through which exchange of gases takes place. • As with photosynthesis, Plant get oxygen from the air through the Stomata. Respiration take place in mitochondria of the cell in the presences of oxygen which is called 'aerobic respiration.'

question: Turpentine oil is obtained from
explanation: Turpentine is a well known essential oil extracted by distillation from pine oleoresin. The oleoresin itself is provided by tapping trees of the genus Pinus. The solid phase left behind after distillation is known as rosin.

question: If all the plants of the world die, all the animals will also die due to the shortage of
explanation: If all plants on earth died, eventually on the living beings on the planet will also die. Human being and other animal need plants to live. When green plants make food they give off oxygen. Oxygen is a gas that all animals need to breathe in order to stay alive.

question: Dioecious species are those which bear

options.a: Male and female flowers on the same plant
options.b: Male and female flowers on separate plants
options.d: Perfect and imperfect flowers on separate plants

explanation: Dioecious species are bear male and female flowers on the same plant. Dioecy is a characteristic of a species that has distinct individual organism that produce male or female gametes, either directly or indirectly.

question: For Thallophyte group of plants includes
explanation: Thallophyta is one amongst the division of the plante. It includes primitive land plants like fungi, lichens and algae. These plants are simple and that they grow without roots, leaves and stems. Science

question: The HIV virus destroys thus weakening the immunity of a person.
explanation: HIV infects and destroys certain white blood cells called CD4+lymphocytes, if too many CD4+cells are destroyed the body can no longer defend itself against infection. The last stage of HIV infection is AIDS (Acquired immune deficiency syndrome).

question: Lichens are symbiotic associations, that is, mutually useful associations, between algae:
explanation: Lichens are symbiotic associations, which are mutually useful between association, Algae and Fungi. The algal component photosynthesize and provide food for the fungus while the fungal component provide protection from the environment by its filament

question: Which causative agent is responsible for spreading swine flu?
explanation: (1) Virus borne diseases– Smallpox, rabies, hepatitis, swine flu, corona. (2) Bacterial diseases– Cholera, Diphtheria, Tuberculosis, Leprosy, Tetanus, Typhoid, Plague, Whooping Cough, Anthrax. (3) Parasitic/Protozoan borne diseases– Malaria, Japanese encephalitis, Dysentery, Black-jar, Pyuria, Trypanosma. (4) Fungal diseases– Athlete's foot, Scabies, Ringworm.

question: Novel Corona Virus is known by its scientific name
explanation: • SARS-COV-2 is the scientific Name given to Novel corona virus by World Health Organization (WHO), on 11 February 2020. • The first case of Novel corona virus was reported from Wuhan province of China in November, 2019. • Corona Virus are a group of related RNA viruses that cause diseases in Mammals and Birds.

question: Ringworm is an infection caused by:
explanation: Ringworm is a common skin infection that is caused by fungus. Some very common types of fungal infections are Athlete's foot, Ringworm, Jock itch etc. • Some common type of virus diseases are Dengue, Chikungunya, Rabies, Covid-19, Nipah, Ebola, AIDS/HIV etc. • Some common type of Protozoan diseases are Malaria, Sleeping Sickness, Kala-azar etc.

question: A type of Coronavirus known as MERS is a term for____ Resiratory Syndrome
explanation: MERS is also called Middle East Respiratory Syndrome. It is and illness caused by virus (more specifically, a Corona virus) called middle East Respiratory syndrome Corona virus (MERS-COV) Most MERS patients developed severe respiratory illness with symptoms of fever, cough and shortness of breath.

question: 'Athlete's foot' is a disease caused by
explanation: 'Athlete's foot' is a disease caused by the same type of fungus that causes ringworm and Jock itch. Damp socks and shoes and warm, humid conditions favor the organism's growth. Bacteria :– Bacteria are a type of biological cell. They constitute a large domain of prokaryotic microorganisms. Typically a few micrometers in length, bacteria have a number of shapes, ranging from sphere to rods and appear and spirals. Bacteria were among the first life forms to appear on earth, and are present in most of its habitats.

question: The Zika Virus is spread by _____.
explanation: Zika Virus disease is caused by a virus transmitted primarily by Aedes mosquitoes, which bite during the day. Symptoms are generally mild and include fever, rash, conjunctivitis, muscle pain and joint pain, malaise or headache. Symptoms typically last for 2-7 days. Most people with Zika virus infection do not develop symptoms. Science

question: Azotobacter is a/an:
explanation: Azotobacter is a Nitrogen Fixing Bacteria. This bacteria is found independently in soil. They convert atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia and this ammonia is obtain as Nitrogen to the roots of plants, hence they are also used as Bio Fertilizer.

question: Mostly antibiotics are obtained from
explanation: Antibiotics are compounds which might be produced through an organism and inhibit the increase of any other organism. They are generally obtained from microorganism like bacteria. They are used to combat bacterial infection, however they are net beneficial in viral infection.

question: Which of the following virus takes its name from a place in Malaysia?
explanation: Nipah virus– It is a bat-borne virus that causes infection in humans and other animals, a disease with a high mortality rate. The Malaysian Government has given the virus name Nipah after the plant used in the construction of traditional malay dwellings. Ebola virus– Ebola is also called as Ebola hemorrhagic fever. A virus that causes severe bleeding & organ failure and can lead to death. Humans may spread the virus to other humans through contact with bodily fluids such as blood. Influenza– Influenza or flu is caused by a virus that leads to respiratory illness. It is highly contagious and spread easily through coughs and sneezes from an infected person. HIV– HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus) is a virus that attacks the body's immune system. If HIV is not treated on time, it can lead to AIDS (Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome).

question: The correct statement(s) about Zika virus epidemic is/are 1. It was originated from forest of Brazil. 2. Vaccination is the only method for protection. 3. It is associated with neurological birth defects in children. 4. It spreads from infected chicken.
explanation: Zika Virus–A disease caused by Zika virus that is spread through the bites of Aedes Aegypti and Aedes Albopictus mosquitoes. It spread through animal or insect bites by having unprotected vaginal, anal or oral sex. In most cases there are no symptoms. In a few cases Zika can trigger paralysis. In pregnant women, it may cause subsequent birth defects. Zika virus was first detected in Uganda in the year 1947 in Rhesus Monkeys and later on in humans in Tanzania in the year 1952. 661 / 896

question: In plants, water is transported through which of the following medium?
explanation: Plants have two systems for the transportation of substances using two types of transport tissue. Xylem transports water and solutes from the roots to the leaves, while phloem transports food from the leaves to the rest of the plants. The Xylem tubes are made from dead Xylem Cells which have the cell walls removed at the end of the cells, forming tubes through which the water and dissolved Mineral ions can flow.

question: In plants, mature ovary changes to which part of the plant?
explanation: In the flowering plants, an ovary is a part of the female reproductive Organ of the plant or Gynoecium's. Specifically, it is the part which contains ovules, which develop into seeds upon fertilization. It will mature into Fruit, either dry and parchment like or freshly, enclosing the seeds.

question: A spore producing plant is………
explanation: Rhizopus stolonifer is commonly known as black bread mould. It is a member of Zygomycota and considered the most important species in the genus Rhizopus. It can produced asexually and sexually. It is a heterothallic species. Spores produced by the bread mould plant are actually its seeds. • Rhizopus Stolonifer is an opportunistic agent of disease and hence will cause infection in people with weakened immunity. Zygomycosis is the main disease caused by fungus.

question: Which of the following multiplies very slowly in comparison to others?
explanation: Multiple fission is a method of fertilisation by asexual reproduction in organism. In this method the parent organism being divided and produces many other new organism. • Viruses, bacteria, and fungi multiply very quickly, while Worms multiply very slowly in comparison.

question: Which of the following processes is used to treat contaminated media by altering environmental conditions to simulate growth of microorganism?
explanation: Bioremediation is a process used to treat contaminated media, including water soil and subsurface material by altering environmental conditions to stimulate growth of microorganisms and degrade the target pollutants. Bioaccumulation– Is the gradual accumulation of substances such as pesticides or other chemicals in an organisms. Bioaugmentation– Biological augmentation is the addition of archea or bacterial cultures required to speed up the rate of degradation of a contaminants.

question: Plants which grow in water of high salinity are called
explanation: Halophytes are salt-resistant or salt tolerant plants and have remarkable ability to complete their cycle in Saline Condition like – Salicornea Europea, Sporobolus alterniforous etc. Only two person of the plant species found on the earth are halophytes. Xerophytes– Growth in little liquid water such as a desert or an ice or snow covered region in Alps or the arctic. Hygrophytes– So much moisture adopting plants. A plant which grows in wet condition. Mesophytes– A plant needing only a moderate amont of water.

question: Antibiotics are useful for which type of infections?
explanation: Antibiotics are also known as antimicrobial drugs. Antibiotics are used to treat or prevent infections caused by bacteria, fungi and protozoa. These medicines treat infections either by killing or decreasing the growth of bacteria. Naturally it is produced by some micro organism or artificially in the laboratory. Penicillin, an antibiotic is used to treat certain infections cause by bacteria. It is discovered by Alexander Flaming.

question: Match the following correctly:
explanation: Beriberi is a disease caused by the deficiency of Vit-B , also known as Thiamine deficiency. 1 • A deficiency of Ascorbic acid (Vitamin C) leads to Scurvy disease. • The chemical name of vitamin D is Calciferol, its deficiency causes Rickets. • A deficiency of Tocopherols (Vitamin E) leads to Sterility. It is also known as beauty Vitamin. 662 / 896

question: 'Nipah' virus gets its name from 'Sungai Nipah', a ______ village.
explanation: 'Nipah' virus was first recognized in 1999 during an outbreak among pig farmers in a Malaysian village. • Nipah virus (NiV) is a zoonotic virus (it is transmitted from animals to humans) and can also be transmitted through contaminated food or directly between people. • In infected people, it causes a range of illnesses from asymptomatic infection to acute respiratory illness and total encephalitis.

question: Penicillin antibiotic was isolated from an organism belonging to group :
explanation: Antibiotics are compounds produced by bacteria and fungi which are capable of the difficult task of isolating pure penicillin from the mold juice. Penicillin discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.

question: The age of a tree can be found by:

options.d: Counting the annual growth rings in a section of its stem

explanation: A tree's age can be determined by counting the annual growth rings in its trunk. Each ring represents one year age of tree and the ring's thickness reveals the relative amount of rainfall that year.

question: Which one of the following biogeochemical cycles is most dependant on bacteria?
explanation: Bacteria plays a key role in the Nitrogen cycle. Nitrogen enters the living world by the way of bacteria and other single-celled prokaryotes, which convert atmospheric nitrogen into biologically usable forms in a process called nitrogen fixation.

question: Nodules in pulses crop produce pink colour due to
explanation: The pink colour in the glands found in the roots of pulses is due to bacteria called Rhizobium. Rhizobium bacteria are found in the roots of leguminous plants and stabilize atmospheric nitrogen. It is the most suitable organic fertilizer for marsh crops.

question: L.S.D. is extracted from which of the following?
explanation: L.S.D. (Lysergic Acid Diethylamide) are hallucinogenic drugs obtained from the sclerotia of Ergot fungus, Claviceps purpurea that grows on rye plant. This fungus produces Ergot alkaloids from which LSD drugs are derived. They are natural hallucinogens and change mood, behavior and thoughts. They affect the cerebrum and sense organs and generate euphoria (excitement). • LSD is not found in lichen, algae and bacteria as they are present in Ergot fungus.

question: The disadvantage of self-pollination is
explanation: The disadvantages of self-pollination come from a lack of variation that allows no adoption to the changing environment or potential pathogen attack. The disadvantage of self- pollination are– (i) The seeds are less in number (ii) The chances of evolution is reduced (iii) Can not be produced new varities of plants.

question: Thigmotropism is the response of the plant to
explanation: Thigmotropism is a movement in which a plant moves or grows in response to touch or contact stimuli. The word 'Thigmotropism' is derived from greek word “Thigmo” means “Touch or contact” and “Tropism” means “Directional”.

question: Decomposers include
explanation: Decomposers include bacteria and fungi. They decompose the waste organic matter from animals and plants and release energy back in the environment.

question: Lichens are a symbiotic association of
explanation: • Lichens are a symbiotic association between a fungus and an algae or a cyanobacterium. • In Lichens, algae prepare food for fungi and fungi provide shelter and absorb mineral nutrients and water for the algae as a partner. • Lichens are environmentally important, helping to create soil, providing food, and acting as indicators of air pollution.

question: The plant which flowers outside and fruit development occurs the soil is
explanation: Groundnut is the plant which flowers grow outside while fruit development occurs in soil. • Gujarat is the largest producer of groundnuts in India, which is followed by Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra and West Bengal.

question: Transpiration would be minimum when there is :
explanation: The transpirations in plants will be minimum when there is high humidity in the atmosphere. The rate of transpiration is directly proportional to the saturation deficit.

question: Which one of the following is not caused by bacteria?
explanation: Measles is caused by a virus in the paramyxovirus family and it is normally passed through direct contact and through the air. The virus infects the respiratory tract, then spreads throughout the body. Measles is a human disease and is not known to occur in animals.

question: Edible part of Ginger is :
explanation: In Ginger the part that we eat is actually the root which is also called as Rhizome of the plant. A rhizome is a modified stem that grows horizontally underground.

question: Which of the following characteristics of virus best denote its unique nature? 1. It can attack bacteria 2. It can be crystallised 3. It can replicate 4. It can invade human body. Select the correct answer using the codes given below Codes:
explanation: Bacteria can be infected by tiny viruses called Bacteriophages. Bacteriophages are so small that they do not even have a single cell, but are instead just a piece of DNA surrounded by a protein coat. (cid:1) The process of transformation of viral components into organized solid particles is known as crystallization. The inactive form of Virus can be changed into crystals. (cid:1) During an infection Viruses invade your cells in order to reproduce.

question: Molds contaminated cereal and grain products are not fit for consumption because of

options.a: The presence of mycotoxin in them
options.b: The presence of phycotoxin in them
options.c: The presence of maximum protein and starch in them

explanation: Molds contaminated cereal and grain products are not fit for consumption because of the presence of mycotoxin in them. We found that grains represent a significant source of food borne contaminants, the main ones being mycotoxin including – aflatoxin B , Ochratocin A, 1 fumonisin B , deoxynivalenol, zearalenone, toxic 1 metals like arsenic, cadmium and leads, as well as process contaminants such as acrylamide.

question: The decomposition and recycling of organic matter from dead organisms is made possible to a large extent by :
explanation: Bacteria are well-known decomposers of dead animal flesh and are efficient at converting animal tissues to simple or organic compounds. Similar to fungi, these organisms also have the capacity to partially break down cellulose into intermediate molecules and facilitate the process of decomposition.

question: Consider the following plants : 1. Cycas 2. Date palm 3. Pinus Which of the above is/are flowering plants :
explanation: Date Palm :- Phoenix dactylifera, commonly known as date or date palm, is a flowering plant species in the Palm family, Arecaceae, cultivated for its edible sweet fruit called dates. Cycas is a genus of plants belonging to a very ancient lineage, the cycadophyta. Pinus Plant :- Pinus is a tree belonging to the group gymnosperms and non-flowering plants that exposed seeds not enclosed in a ovary. Note : Cycas and Pinus are not flowering plants

question: The ginger is a
explanation: Ginger is a Rhizome. Rhizome is a modified subterranean stem of a plant that is usually found under ground, after sending out roots and shoots from its nodes. Examples of rhizome plants are Asparagus, Ginger, Irises Lily of the valley etc.

explanation: Mendel's monohybrid ratio – Trait : Seed shape Alleles : R-Round r-wrinkled Cross : Round seeds X-round seeds Rr × Rr 664 / 896

explanation: Rhizobium is a bacterium formed in soil that helps fixing nitrogen in leguminous plants and produced NH which absorbed by plants. These nodules fix atmospheric nitrogen and convert it into ammonia that can be used by the plant for its growth and development.

question: Tetanus is caused by :
